title: One Person Killed After 7.4 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Indonesia | 10 News
description: At least one person has died and several buildings have been damaged after a magnitude 7.4 earthquake struck in the Molucca Sea off Indonesia's Ternate Island on Thursday.  The quake, which struck at 06:48 local time at a depth of 35km, sparked tsunami warnings along the coasts of Indonesia, the Philippines and Malaysia which have since been withdrawn.  Local media reported a 70-year-old woman died in North Sulawesi after being crushed by building debris, and another person broke their leg after jumping off a building. title: Gambling Ads Banned During Live Sport In Major Reform | 10 News
description: Gambling ads will be banned from sporting events, including during live broadcasts, in what the Prime Minister says are "the most significant gambling reforms" in Australia's history.   It comes three years after a parliamentary inquiry pushed for a total ban with critics accusing Anthony Albanese of caving to gaming lobbyists. title: Alleged Bondi Gunman Naveed Akram Denied Suppression Order On Family Identities | 10 News
description: Alleged Bondi terrorist Naveed Akram has failed in his bid to suppress the identity of his mother, brother and sister, after they received death threats in the wake of the December 14 massacre. The court found the identities of the family members have already been widely reported internationally and their safety fears weren't enough to warrant protection. title: Are You Really Allergic To Penicillin? | 10 News
description: A world-first study by Australian scientists is forcing a rethink about one of the most common drug allergies.  The research has found as many as 95 per cent of people told they're allergic to penicillin are probably not. title: Key Aussie Role In NASA's Historic Mission, Crisis In The Strait, Fuel Prices Fall AGAIN | 10 News+
description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has been forced to defend his decision to hold a national address amid the ongoing fuel crisis and the Iran war, with many claiming it was a waste of time and that it sparked panic buying.  Terrifying footage has emerged of oil tankers being struck at sea, as an estimated 20,000 seafarers have been trapped for more than a month in the Persian Gulf amid the ongoing conflict with Iran.  As the first crewed lunar mission in 50 years takes astronauts further from Earth than humans have ever travelled before, Australia will play a crucial role in keeping them connected with 'The Dish'.  A series of ‘racy’ text messages between Prince Harry and a reporter has put a huge question mark over the royal’s previous claim that he has never spoken privately to reporters.
